column1: #Text=The barn swallow is the most widespread species of swallow in the world, occurring on all continents, with vagrants reported even in Antarctica.
#Text=It is a distinctive passerine bird with blue upperparts and a long, deeply forked tail.
#Text=In Anglophone Europe, it is just called the swallow; in northern Europe, it is the only member of family Hirundinidae called a "swallow" rather than a "martin".
